title: Enterprise Service Bus: an SOA Middleware Foundation
author: Aberdeen Group
subject_domain: ESB; SOA middleware; enterprise application integration; SOA adoption
methodology: survey; benchmark; PACE framework; qualitative interviews; 2006
abstract: 38-page Aberdeen Group benchmark report sponsored by Fiorano and IBM. Part of 'Next Steps in SOA Series'. ESB as messaging middleware for SOA. Survey findings on ESB adoption patterns across company sizes. Three SOA camps identified: SOA Lite, Enterprise SOA, SOA ERP. 90% of respondents will exit 2006 with SOA experience. ESB adoption concentrated in enterprises >$500M revenue. BEA Systems top primary ESB vendor (57% BIC), IBM WebSphere second (37%), webMethods third. Creates context for EAI-to-ESB transition. Covers hardware server forecasts driven by ESB deployment. Aberdeen PACE framework applied.
importance_rationale: First major benchmark quantifying ESB market with vendor rankings, company-size segmentation, and business value calculation ($143B annual opportunity); defines ESB's role in SOA infrastructure
relevance_rationale: Comprehensive survey data on SOA/ESB adoption rates, vendor market share, deployment challenges, buying criteria, and company-size segmentation; highly actionable
prescience_rationale: Correctly predicted ESB would complement not replace EAI; anticipated hardware spend driven by SOA XML overhead; SOA suite approach now standard in API management platforms
